title: Penducella werneri
intro: Penducella werneri, also known as Mexican pennyroyal, is a plant species in the mint family that is native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its ability to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as bloating and gas, as well as its potential to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Penducella werneri include essential oils, primarily consisting of pulegone and menthone, as well as other compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Mexican pennyroyal is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often consumed to promote digestive health and alleviate stress.
taxonomy: Penducella werneri is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Penducella and is native to South Africa. The species is further classified as a member of the subfamily Carduoideae. Penducella werneri is closely related to other species within the Carduoideae subfamily, which includes thistles and similar-looking plants. The exact classification of Penducella werneri may vary depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic studies.
